messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2026 Streaming
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2026 Streaming
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,
Can't join EuroBioC2026 in person? We've got you covered.
All conference sessions will be streamed live and are free to attend online. Each session room has its own streaming link, so be sure to use the appropriate link for the session you would like to follow.
You can find all streaming links here:
https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/pages/streaming.html#streaming
Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Can't join EuroBioC2026 in person? We've got you covered.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Landing page does not match the current version of my package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Landing page does not match the current version of my package
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I don't think the message is being allowed through because of the images referenced/included. The bioc-devel mailing list does not allow image attachments.

For others can reference the original question:
Why do the landing page versions of my package at bioconductor.org  do not match the checktable at Runiverse page?

We do not use R-universe yet as the default build for packages; that is in process. We still have our linux builders
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/.  We pull windows and some mac products from R-universe but compared to the passing version of our linux builder as there are certain Bioconductor criteria that is being checked and processed on the linux builders that we have not yet implemented on R-universe only builds.

The build reports and landing pages are lagging as there was an issue with the post processing infrastructure on our end.  We apologize that we did not announce this more broadly on the mailing list for reference and to avoid confusion; we will make sure to do this in the future.  We have implemented a fix and expect the builds to resume and update within the next 24 hours.  Thank you for your patience and understanding.

We appreciate your diligence to the Bioconductor builds and clarification question.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.body_plain:
Dear BioC,

We would like to change the maintainer for the MoonlightR package from me to Antonio Colaprico (antonio.colaprico at curie.fr<mailto:antonio.colaprico at curie.fr> ; cc?d).

What do we need to do to make this happen? I assume we need confirmation form Antonio at the very least (he should already be part of BioConductor)

I know I?m supposed to request the change here, but I am a bit fuzzy on the details:

  1.
I assume we need to change the maintainer role in the DESCRIPTION file - does this need to be done immediately both in the release and devel branch? And before or after the official change?
  2.
Once you do the switch, will the change be effective for both branches immediately?

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for reaching out.

We can proceed with this request as the current maintainer.  Please have Antonio confirm and we can make this change on the configuration on our end.

We recommend Antonio make the change once we finish up change the configuration as then they can test that change of access was successful.

Once the configuration on our end is changed, it is immediate on both branches.


Antonio,
I do not see a BiocCredentials account yet for you.  We will need to create this in order to update the configuration.  Please let us know what email you would like to use for login and your github username. We will send activation email directly to you. Once activated you will be able to manage ssh keys for access.


Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

We would like to change the maintainer for the MoonlightR package from me to Antonio Colaprico (antonio.colaprico at curie.fr<mailto:antonio.colaprico at curie.fr> ; cc?d).

What do we need to do to make this happen? I assume we need confirmation form Antonio at the very least (he should already be part of BioConductor)

I know I?m supposed to request the change here, but I am a bit fuzzy on the details:

  1.
I assume we need to change the maintainer role in the DESCRIPTION file - does this need to be done immediately both in the release and devel branch? And before or after the official change?
  2.
Once you do the switch, will the change be effective for both branches immediately?

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: BioC2026 Early Bird Registration Closes June 15
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: BioC2026 Early Bird Registration Closes June 15
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,
This is a reminder that early bird registration for BioC2026 is open until June 15, 2026.
BioC2026 will take place in Seattle this August and will bring together members of the Bioconductor community for scientific presentations, workshops, networking, and discussions on advances in computational biology and bioinformatics.
?? Secure your spot NOW: https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/registration/
Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Interested in VariantAnnotation transfer and BiocStyle assistance
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Interested in VariantAnnotation transfer and BiocStyle assistance
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Community,



I am writing to express my interest in both open maintainership opportunities currently listed on the Help Wanted page:



- VariantAnnotation - Transfer

- BiocStyle - Assistance



I am John Muirhead-Gould, founder of Nodes Bio ( https://nodes.bio ), a bioinformatics platform built in part on the Bioconductor ecosystem. Both packages are directly relevant to our work and our users.



VariantAnnotation (Transfer)

I have reviewed the full codebase and the 30-issue open backlog. My immediate priorities would be:

- Correctness fixes in locateVariants and predictCoding (#81, #83, #84, #86)

- I/O round-trip fidelity (#78, #80, #65)

- EnsDb support in locateVariants (#74) - the single most requested enhancement

- Expanded Ensembl VEP REST API integration, building on the existing use_vep_api.R foundation



BiocStyle (Assistance)

BiocStyle is infrastructure every Bioconductor package depends on for documentation. I am happy to take on the maintenance burden here - responding to issues, keeping pace with Pandoc and rmarkdown changes, and ensuring the package stays green across release cycles.



I am comfortable with R, C, the Bioconductor release cycle, and the support obligations that come with core package maintenance.



Please let me know what steps are needed to proceed on both. Happy to provide any additional information or references.



Best regards,



John Muirhead-Gould

Founder | Nodes Bio, Inc.

j

https://www.nodes.bio 

330-206-3796
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

ich bin bis zum 26.06.2026 nicht im Institut und habe nur eingeschr?nkte M?glichkeit meine Mails abzurufen. 

Dear Sir/Madam,
I am out of office until June 26th, 2026.
In this time I will have only limited access to my e-mails. 

Mit freundlichen Gr??en/Best regards

Mariano Ruz Jurado

> 
> Matteo Tiberti

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.body_plain:
Dear Lori,
Thank you for your email and for your help with this transition.
First of all, my apologies for the delay in getting back to you.
I confirm that I will take over as the maintainer of the MoonlightR package and would like to proceed with the requested change.
For the BiocCredentials account, please use my personal email address:
antonio.colaprico at gmail.com<mailto:antonio.colaprico at gmail.com>
My GitHub username is:
torongs82
Once the configuration has been updated on your end and the account is activated, I will update the package configuration and test that the access change was successful.
Please let me know if you need any additional information or if there is anything else I should do on my side.
Thank you again for your support.
Best regards,
Antonio Colaprico, PhD
Institut Curie, Paris, France

________________________________
From: Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org>
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 13:15
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>; Matteo Tiberti <tiberti at cancer.dk>
Cc: Colaprico Antonio <antonio.colaprico at curie.fr>
Subject: Re: Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package

Thank you for reaching out.

We can proceed with this request as the current maintainer.  Please have Antonio confirm and we can make this change on the configuration on our end.

We recommend Antonio make the change once we finish up change the configuration as then they can test that change of access was successful.

Once the configuration on our end is changed, it is immediate on both branches.


Antonio,
I do not see a BiocCredentials account yet for you.  We will need to create this in order to update the configuration.  Please let us know what email you would like to use for login and your github username. We will send activation email directly to you. Once activated you will be able to manage ssh keys for access.


Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Matteo Tiberti via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 3:25 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Cc: Colaprico Antonio <antonio.colaprico at curie.fr>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package


Dear BioC,

We would like to change the maintainer for the MoonlightR package from me to Antonio Colaprico (antonio.colaprico at curie.fr<mailto:antonio.colaprico at curie.fr> ; cc?d).

What do we need to do to make this happen? I assume we need confirmation form Antonio at the very least (he should already be part of BioConductor)

I know I?m supposed to request the change here, but I am a bit fuzzy on the details:

  1.
I assume we need to change the maintainer role in the DESCRIPTION file - does this need to be done immediately both in the release and devel branch? And before or after the official change?
  2.
Once you do the switch, will the change be effective for both branches immediately?

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]


This email message may contain legally privileged and/or confidential information. If you are not the intended recipient(s), or the employee or agent responsible for the delivery of this message to the intended recipient(s), you are hereby notified that any disclosure, copying, distribution, or use of this email message is prohibited. If you have received this message in error, please notify the sender immediately by e-mail and delete this email message from your computer. Thank you.

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] June Developer Forum
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] June Developer Forum
messages.body_plain:
Hi folks,

Our June Developer Forum will be held this Thursday at 4pm UTC! We have Szabolcs Horv?t presenting on:

 igraph and supporting complex graph analysis tools across multiple programming languages.

A registration link is included below. I look forward to seeing y'all there! If you would like to present in the future, or have a topic request feel free to reach out to me.


<https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/gIOto0duQ_y3kU7qEzQeOg>
Welcome! You are invited to join a meeting: June Developer Forum. After registering, you will receive a confirmation email about joining the meeting.<https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/gIOto0duQ_y3kU7qEzQeOg>
us06web.zoom.us<https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/gIOto0duQ_y3kU7qEzQeOg>
[zoom.ico]<https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/gIOto0duQ_y3kU7qEzQeOg>

Cheers,
Nick

Nicholas Cooley, PhD (he/him)
Developer Engagement Lead
Bioconductor / University of Limerick
